question: Q3
claim: Per-branch durable status ledger exists (orchestration-status.log, started/completed/failed JSONL with duration_ms) but is observability-only, not retry state.

question: Q3
claim: No retry and no transient/fatal classification: settleItem records a single terminal rejected result, runCappedPool dispatches each item exactly once with no requeue.

question: Q3
claim: Worker collapses every non-zero/timed-out CLI exit into one undifferentiated throw; timedOut (SIGTERM) is the only distinguishing flag and it does not gate a retry.

question: Q3
claim: Recovery is post-hoc file salvage only (reconstruct missing iteration .md from stdout); it cannot re-execute a branch.

question: Q3
claim: A transiently-failed lineage can only be recovered by re-running the whole fan-out; the ledger has per-label failed events but nothing consumes them as resumable state.

question: Q3
claim: aionforge classifies Transient vs Fatal and reads attempt count from a durable audit trail (max_retries=5), so a crash does not reset the retry budget.

question: Q4
claim: Cost guards are advisory count caps: evaluateCouncilCostGuards only returns continue_allowed/stop_reasons and never aborts/kills/throws; honoring the stop is the caller's job. No hard kill-switch.

question: Q4
claim: Only signals are static counts + a verdict-delta saturation check; no live lag/in-flight-pending/failure-rate gauge metering actual consumption against the pre-computed upper bound.

question: Q4
claim: Fan-out ledger already records per-lineage duration_ms + started/completed/failed counts — the raw material for backpressure gauges — but only summarized post-run, never sampled live to trip a ceiling.

question: Q4
claim: Budget model is count-to-cap-and-hope-caller-stops; K lineages each running to the 4h timeout consume full budget with zero mid-flight lag/pending warning.

question: Q4
claim: aionforge emits live consolidation_lag_seconds/episodes_pending/episodes_failed gauges + per-tick counters and WARNS when oldest-pending age exceeds lag_ceiling (default 5s) — observable backpressure with a tripwire.

question: Q5
claim: prompt-pack.ts is a pure checked-placeholder renderer ({var} substitution, throw on missing token) with zero iteration/finding/open-question awareness; continuity is entirely whatever the caller puts in the variables map.

question: Q5
claim: Only callers of renderPromptPack are the module + its vitest; cross-iteration continuity is carried by the reducer folding findings into strategy.md anchors that the next iteration re-reads cold, not by a threaded reflection turn.

question: Q5
claim: galadriel ambient reflection is the threading model: a silent side-effect-only turn that takes stock of open questions/changed facts and files them; explicit next stage is reflection that reads its own diary + open-questions and threads across ticks rather than starting fresh.

question: Q5
claim: Deep-loop iterations thread forward only via the reducer's distilled strategy.md anchors, reconstructing their working set from the snapshot rather than threading their own raw prior open-questions — the reactive reconstruct-cold mode galadriel threading improves on.

question: Q1
claim: upsert.cjs threads a free-form metadata field onto both nodes and edges through to batchUpsert; weight (clampWeight) is the only structured numeric edge attribute. A Q1 Beta-posterior reliability term has a no-migration landing slot (metadata or weight), though no current writer populates a reliability value.

question: Q3
note: Candidate (M): consume ledger per-label failed events as resumable state + transient/fatal classification keyed on timedOut/exit-code + durable per-branch attempt count, re-dispatch one branch instead of re-running all siblings. Smaller (S): surface a retryable flag in summary without auto-retry.

question: Q4
note: Candidate (M): live backpressure gauges (in-flight, oldest-pending lag, failure rate) sampled from existing started/completed/failed ledger events + enforceable lag_ceiling tripwire honored as hard stop. Smaller (S): emit lag_seconds/pending gauge line per tick for observability parity, no enforcement.

question: Q5
note: Candidate (S): each iteration emits a lightweight self-owned open-questions-carried-forward block (separate from reducer machine-owned fold) so next iteration threads its own thread cheaply; reuses existing JSONL/strategy spine, no new model call.

Both gaps share the same untapped substrate: the fan-out orchestration-status.log ledger already holds the per-branch durable data (retry state for Q3, backpressure gauges for Q4) but nothing reads it live.
Free-form metadata + weight slot is the no-migration landing spot for the iter-3 Q1 Beta-posterior reliability term.
